forecasts.reasoning: The climate.mil portal was removed from the Department of Defense website in early 2025 by the Trump administration as part of a broader removal of climate-related information from federal websites. As of May 5, 2025, IsItDownRightNow confirmed the site was still down. There is no news or evidence indicating any plans or actions by the administration to reverse course and restore the site by July 1, 2025. Given the administration's clear policy directives against climate initiatives, the probability of the site unexpectedly coming back online within two months is exceedingly low.

The involvement of HHS Secretary RFK Jr., who specifically directed the CDC in April 2025 to review and halt its fluoride recommendation, strongly suggests a change was imminent. While typical government reviews take years, the new administration demonstrated rapid action when the FDA moved to remove prescription fluoride supplements from the market by May 2025. This shows a high willingness to execute anti-fluoride policies quickly. However, a KFF article in August 2025 still referred to the "current HHS/USPHS guidelines" recommending 0.7 mg/L, suggesting the formal recommendation may still technically be on the books or the website may not have been fully purged of the recommendation by July 1, 2025. Given the explicit pressure from HHS, it is more likely than not that the CDC's official recommendation page was pulled, suspended, or modified by the July 1 deadline, but bureaucratic inertia keeps the YES probability non-trivial.

Before July 1, 2025, there is no direct evidence that President Trump issued a presidential action explicitly containing the exact phrase "Diversity Visa." Major regulatory changes to the program, such as introducing a passport requirement or a $1 fee, occurred in August and September 2025, respectively. Additionally, the major action to suspend the program happened in December 2025 following a highly publicized incident at Brown University. Early executive actions in January 2025 regarding immigration (e.g., reviving the travel ban) and DEI programs did occasionally spark commentary on their potential effect on the Diversity Visa program, but there is no record of the exact phrase being invoked directly in a published Presidential Action on whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/ prior to July 1, 2025. The strict resolution criteria ("exactly those words") further reduces the likelihood, as official documents often use "Diversity Immigrant Visa" or other statutory terminology. Thus, I assign a low probability.

The resolution date is July 1, 2025. Search results confirm that while there were pushes to ban junk food/soda in early 2025, the USDA did not actually approve the first major batch of state waivers to restrict these items until August 6, 2025. Additionally, new federal rules that proposed terminating certain snack foods from counting as 'staple foods' were not reported until October 2025. Federal agency websites do not proactively remove explicit eligibility categories before waivers or overarching rules are even finalized. Furthermore, news articles and institutional sources from late 2025 and early 2026 continue to explicitly quote 'snack foods and non-alcoholic beverages' as SNAP-eligible items, strongly implying the core USDA text had not simply been deleted. Given that all major regulatory action occurred after July 1, 2025, the page almost certainly still listed 'snack foods' when checked on the resolution date.

The resolution date is July 1, 2025. The Trump administration did not suspend the Diversity Visa program until December 18, 2025. Furthermore, documents from August 2025 (Federal Register) and Reddit posts from July 2025 clearly refer to the exact URL in question ('travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/us-visas/immigrate/diversity-visa-program-entry/diversity-visa-submit-entry1.html'). This confirms the page and URL structure were maintained past the July 1, 2025 resolution date, satisfying the resolution criteria.
